compiled_state.title: As the fix-bug orchestrator, I validate hypothesis_tests before allowing fix implementation
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Add validation logic to the fix-bug orchestrator that checks the investigation RESULT for at least one hypothesis_tests entry with verdict=confirmed before proceeding to fix implementation.
**Why**: Even with the schema in place (story 78aa-79b2), the orchestrator must enforce the gate — rejecting results without confirmed hypotheses and escalating to the next tier.
**Scope**:
- IN: Validation between Step 2 (investigation) and Step 6 (fix implementation), escalation on missing/disproved hypotheses
- OUT: RED-before-fix gate (story b094-3cf4), unit tests (story 8a52-74f7)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the fix-bug orchestrator rejects investigation results that have no hypothesis_tests entries and escalates to the next investigation tier
  ← Satisfies: "The fix-bug orchestrator validates that the investigation result contains at least one hypothesis_tests entry with verdict=confirmed"
- When this story is complete, the orchestrator rejects investigation results where all hypothesis_tests entries have verdict=disproved and escalates
  ← Satisfies: "if all hypotheses are disproved or the section is missing, the orchestrator escalates"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Escalation must follow existing tier path (BASIC → INTERMEDIATE → ADVANCED → ESCALATED → User)
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 2/6: Code patterns understood ✓ — investigation results processed between Step 2 and Step 6. Escalation path: BASIC → INTERMEDIATE → ADVANCED → ESCALATED → User. Existing escalation triggers in Step 5 (no RED test) and Step 7 (fix fails). Need to add gate after Step 2 return, before Step 3/4/5/6.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 3/6: Tests written ✓ — 5 RED tests added to tests/skills/test_fix_bug_skill.py class TestHypothesisValidationGate: gate present, escalate on missing, escalate on all-disproved, proceed on confirmed, escalate to next tier.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 4/6: Implementation complete ✓ — Added Step 3.5: Hypothesis Validation Gate to SKILL.md between Step 3 (hypothesis testing) and Step 4 (fix approval). Gate checks for missing/empty hypothesis_tests → escalate, all-disproved verdicts → escalate, at least one verdict=confirmed → proceed.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 5/6: Tests passing ✓ — python3 -m pytest tests/skills/test_fix_bug_skill.py: 43 passed. bash tests/hooks/test-fix-bug-skill.sh: PASSED: 12  FAILED: 0.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 6/6: Done ✓ — AC1 (escalate on missing/empty): satisfied by gate logic item 1. AC2 (escalate on all-disproved): satisfied by gate logic item 2. AC3 (unit tests passing): 5 new tests in TestHypothesisValidationGate, all GREEN.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Fixed: Added Step 3.5 Hypothesis Validation Gate to fix-bug SKILL.md. Gate validates hypothesis_tests entries, escalates on missing/all-disproved, proceeds on confirmed verdict. 5 tests pass.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
